# yttoc — YouTube Xscript to Structured Table of Contents

> nbdev-based Python CLI that downloads YouTube captions (ja preferred, en fallback),
> parses SRT into normalized xscript segments, and generates structured ToC and summaries
> via LLM. Converts video transcripts into searchable, hierarchical knowledge assets.

<!-- BEGIN AUTO -->
## Project

- [README.md](README.md): Auto-generated README with install instructions and nbdev dev guide
- [CLAUDE.md](CLAUDE.md): Claude Code guidance — project overview, xscript terminology, nbdev conventions
- [AGENTS.md](AGENTS.md): Repo-specific dev rules, notebook-module map, and build commands for nbdev workflow

## Design & Data

- [docs/implementation-plan.md](docs/implementation-plan.md): Phased roadmap from SRT parsing to RAG, with CLI design and module map
- [docs/reference-tocs.md](docs/reference-tocs.md): Hand-curated ToCs for evaluating yttoc output quality
- [docs/videos.md](docs/videos.md): Target video list — Jeremy Howard's Solveit series (~23 hours)
<!-- END AUTO -->
